title: Minimum Number of Arrows to Burst Balloons
problem_statement: Find the minimum number of arrows required to burst all balloons represented by horizontal intervals.

examples.explanation: One arrow at x = 6 bursts [2,8] and [1,6], another at x = 11 bursts [10,16] and [7,12].

intuition: Sort by interval end and greedily shoot arrows at the earliest possible end point without missing overlapping intervals.

approaches.name: Greedy interval scheduling
approaches.description: Sort balloons by end coordinate, place an arrow at each interval end, and skip all intervals that overlap with that arrow.
approaches.why_not: Brute force checking all arrow placements is too expensive; the greedy choice on interval end is optimal for minimizing arrows.

interview_qa.a: It allows one arrow to cover the maximum number of overlapping intervals by shooting at the earliest finish point.
Each non-overlapping balloon requires a separate arrow.
